info.title: Leywn
info.description:
Last Echo You Will Need — an all-in-one demo backend for APIs and services.

All endpoints support content negotiation: include `Accept: application/xml` to receive XML instead of the default JSON. Set `LEYWN_ONLY_JSON=true` to disable XML and always return JSON.

CORS headers are added to every response. Set `LEYWN_CORS_ORIGIN` to restrict the allowed origin (default: `*`).

tags.name: Auth
tags.description: Authentication demo endpoints (Basic Auth, API Key, JWT, mTLS)

tags.name: Random
tags.description: Random value generators (integers, UUIDs, Lorem Ipsum, names, emails, colors)

tags.name: Info
tags.description: Caller information and server time endpoints

tags.name: Utility
tags.description: Utility endpoints (status codes, images, delay, stream, health)

tags.name: Chaos
tags.description: Chaos engineering endpoint — injects errors, mangled responses, and latency

get.summary: Exchange JWT for a Leywn-signed token (Bearer)

200.description: A new HS256 JWT signed by Leywn (Bearer mode)

get.description: Validates the incoming Bearer JWT (structure only, not signature) and issues a new HS256-signed token with the original claims plus iss, iat, and jti.

post.summary: Exchange JWT — Bearer or RFC 8693 Token Exchange
post.description:
Supports two exchange modes:

**Bearer mode** (default): send `Authorization: Bearer <token>` with any Content-Type. Returns the full echo payload plus `exchanged_token` and `claims`.

**RFC 8693 mode**: POST with `Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded` and `grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:token-exchange`. Returns a standards-compliant token exchange response with `access_token`, `issued_token_type`, `token_type`, and `expires_in`.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer JWT (used in Bearer mode; omit when using RFC 8693 form body)

requestBody.description: RFC 8693 Token Exchange request body (use instead of Authorization header)

get.description: Validates the TLS client certificate against the Leywn CA. Use the HTTPS port (4443) or set LEYWN_MTLS_IN_HEADER to pass the cert in a header.

audience.description: Intended audience; added as aud claim in the issued token

scope.description: Space-delimited scopes; added as scope claim in the issued token
